name: 1988 Mass Executions of Political Prisoners
description: In the summer of 1988, following the end of the [Iran-Iraq War](/events/iran-iraq-war), the Islamic Republic carried out the mass execution of thousands of political prisoners across Iran. The killings were ordered by a fatwa from [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) and carried out by 'Death Commissions' (committees of three or four officials) in prisons throughout the country. Estimates of those killed range from 2,800 to 30,000. [Ebrahim Raisi](/people/ebrahim-raisi) is alleged to have served on the Tehran Death Commission. The executions targeted primarily members of the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq) and leftist groups.
editorial_notes: One of the most significant human rights atrocities of the Islamic Republic.
